questions.question: Which regions does orion-api run in?
How long are logs stored by default and what command archives them?
questions.gold: Logs are stored 7 days by default; 'orion archive' moves them to long-term storage.

questions.question: What is the current orion-api version in eu-west-1?
questions.gold: v2.4.0, deployed after a VPN-pin fix in trajectory 01.

questions.question: What is the current orion-worker version in us-east-1?
questions.gold: v2.5.0, after the rollback that fixed the message-drop incident in trajectory 02.

questions.question: What is the current orion-dashboard version in us-east-1?
questions.gold: v1.9.0, deployed at 02:14 UTC during the allowed deploy window in trajectory 03.

questions.question: What is the correct sequence to deploy orion-api to eu-west-1?
questions.gold: Run 'orion vpn-pin eu-west-1' first, then 'orion deploy orion-api eu-west-1 <version>'.

questions.question: How do you fully roll back an orion-api deploy, including config?
questions.gold: Run 'orion rollback <svc> <region>' for the binary, then 'orion config restore <svc> <region> <version>' for the config.

questions.question: How do you deploy orion-dashboard if it is currently 14:00 UTC?
questions.gold: Use --schedule with a time inside the 00:00-04:00 UTC deploy window; the deploy will execute then.

questions.question: Why might an eu-west-1 deploy of orion-api appear submitted but never become live?
questions.gold: Because VPN is not pinned to eu-west-1, auth fails silently after submission and the deploy gets rolled back.

questions.question: Why might orion-api still serve 5xx errors after a 'successful' rollback?
questions.gold: Because 'orion rollback' rolls back the binary only; the config is left at the newer schema and must be restored with 'orion config restore'.

questions.question: Why might orion-worker start dropping queue messages without an obvious cause?
questions.gold: Because orion-worker exceeds orion-api by more than 1 minor version; messages get dropped until the worker is rolled back.

questions.question: Why is multi-region routing in OrionDeploy hardcoded in YAML rather than service-discovered?
questions.gold: Because the system predates multi-region as a design goal; routing.yaml is the original mechanism and was never migrated.

questions.question: Why doesn't the orion CLI expose any feature flag operations?
questions.gold: Feature flags live in a separate service (orion-flags) shared across all services; flag operations are intentionally outside this CLI.

questions.question: If you toggle a flag in orion-flags, which services are affected?
questions.gold: All three orion services — orion-api, orion-worker, and orion-dashboard — because orion-flags is shared across them.
